mnemonics.title: SCHEDULING CRITERIA MEMORIZER
mnemonics.content: CUPS WAIT - CPU Utilization, Throughput, Turnaround time, Response time, Waiting time. REMEMBER: CUPS serves PROCESSES while they WAIT

mnemonics.title: FCFS ISSUES
mnemonics.content: CONVOY - Convoy effect occurs, O(n) overhead, No priority, Very simple, Yields poor response for short jobs

mnemonics.title: SJF PROBLEM
mnemonics.content: STARVE - Starvation possible, Time unknown, Accurate prediction difficult, Requires future knowledge, Running time estimation needed, Expensive to implement

acronyms.usage: Simplest scheduling algorithm serving processes in arrival order
Algorithm that selects process with minimum burst time
Advanced scheduling with dynamic priority adjustment
The main processor that executes processes

visualTips.visualization: Think of a BUS STAND where passengers (processes) wait in line. The first person in line gets on the bus (CPU) first. If there's a time limit per person, it's like Round Robin.
Imagine a TEACHER switching between different students answering questions. Each switch takes time to save one student's work and pull out the next student's work.
Think of a ROTATING RESTAURANT where each table gets exactly 20 minutes before being asked to leave for the next table. Tables (processes) keep rotating through.
Think of an ELEVATOR where people waiting longer gradually get higher priority. Someone waiting 5 minutes gets served before someone who just arrived but has lower priority.

analogies.analogy: SCHEDULING IS LIKE A RESTAURANT KITCHEN. The head chef (CPU) needs to prepare dishes (processes) coming from customers. The maitre d' (scheduler) decides which dish gets cooked when, balancing quick dishes for impatient customers (interactive processes) with complex dishes (batch processes).

ROUND ROBIN IS LIKE A ROUND-ROBIN TOURNAMENT where each team (process) gets equal playing time (time quantum) before rotation. Even if a team could use more time, they must pass the ball to the next team.
SJF IS LIKE A EXPRESS CHECKOUT LANE for items under 10, while FCFS IS LIKE A SINGLE REGULAR LINE where everyone waits their turn regardless of how many items they have.
STARVATION IS LIKE A RESTAURANT where VIP customers (high priority) keep arriving, and regular customers (low priority) never get a table because there's always a VIP waiting.
